popularStory.headline: Martim Vasques da Cunha
popularStory.description: Foi o 4º senhor do morgado de Tábua, tendo nascido c. 1235. Alcaide-mor do Castelo de Linhares da Beira. No reinado de D. Dinis, foi alcaide-mor do Castelo de Celorico de Basto. Senhor de Pombeiro, An …

zh.Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal: 葡萄牙布拉加布拉加庫尼亞
zh.lifeSummary: 當Martim Vasques da Cunha於1235年出生在葡萄牙可布拉時，他的父親Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua40歲，母親Teresa Pires Portel25歲。他和Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães至少有5個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1305年在他的家鄉享年70歲，埋葬在葡萄牙可布拉。

zh-hans.Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal: 葡萄牙布拉加布拉加庫尼亞
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Martim Vasques da Cunha于1235年出生在葡萄牙可布拉时，他的父亲Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua40岁，母亲Teresa Pires Portel25岁。他和Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães至少有5个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1305年在他的家乡享年70岁，埋葬在葡萄牙可布拉。

nameMeanings.name: Vasques
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name   Vasco  .

variants.name: Vasco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Fernando, Angel, Arnando, Arturo, Carlos, Dominga, Enrique, Jairo, Luis, Luz, Sergio.  Italian Carmine, Dante, Giro, Giuseppe, Michelangelo, Nunzi.

Spanish and Italian: ethnic name from vasco ‘Basque’, denoting someone from the Basque Country, or from a Spanish byname or Italian personal name of the same derivation. The inhabitants of this region, which extends between southern France and northern Spain, are first recorded in Latin sources as the Vascones, a word of unexplained etymology. See also Gascoigne . The Basques’ own name for themselves is Euskaldun. Compare Basco .
Spanish and Portuguese: old personal name corresponding to   Velasco  .

nameMeanings.name: da
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 達, said to be derived from the personal name Shu Da (叔達), a talent during the reign of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437  BC ); or adopted as a Han surname by other minority ethnic groups.
Mandarin form of the surname 笪, a surname originating in ancient Jianzhou (located in present-day Fujian province) and Jurong (located in Jiangsu province) or a surname from the Hui ethnic group.

nameMeanings.name: Cunha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Mario, Luiz, Fernando, Ricardo, Domingos, Eduardo, Jorge, Alberto, Americo.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo, Agostinho, Albano, Conceicao, Henrique, Margarida, Vasco, Zulmira.
Portuguese: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cunha; the ancient form of the placename is Cuinha, probably meaning ‘kitchen’ (from Latin culina). Compare Da Cunha .

variants.name: Cuna
meanings: Italian (mainly Apulia): perhaps a nickname from cuna ‘crib’.
Portuguese: nickname related to cuna ‘crib’, or perhaps a variant of Cunha .

variants.name: Da Cunha
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Afonso, Albano, Almir, Ilidio, Serafim.  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Mario, Carlos, Alvaro, Fernando, Jorge, Rogerio, Alfonso, Alfredo, Armando, Armindo.
Portuguese: variant of Cunha , with the addition of da, a shortened form of the preposition de ‘from’ and the feminine definite article a ‘the’.

nameMeanings.name: Martim
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Latin name Martinus. This was probably originally derived from Mars (genitive Martis), the name of the Roman god of war (and earlier of fertility). Martin became very popular in the Middle Ages, especially on the Continent, as a result of the fame of St Martin of Tours . He was born the son of a Roman officer in Upper Pannonia (an outpost of the Roman Empire, now part of Hungary), and, although he became a leading figure in the 4th-century Church, he is chiefly remembered now for having divided his cloak in two and given half to a beggar. The name was also borne by five popes, including one who defended Roman Catholic dogma against Eastern Orthodox theology. He died after suffering imprisonment and privations in Naxos and public humiliation in Constantinople, and was promptly acclaimed a martyr by supporters of the Roman Church. Among Protestants, the name is sometimes bestowed in honour of the German theologian Martin Luther ( 1483–1546 ); Martin was used as a symbolic name for the Protestant Church in satires by both Dryden and Swift. A further influence may be its use as the given name of the civil-rights leader Martin Luther King ( 1929–68 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Martim Vasques da Cunha was born in 1235, in Coimbra, Portugal, his father, Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua, was 40 and his mother, Teresa Pires Portel, was 25. He had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães. He died in 1305, in his hometown, at the age of 70, and was buried in Coimbra, Portugal.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Martim Vasques da Cunha est né en 1235, à Coimbra, Portugal, son père, Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Teresa Pires Portel, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães. Il est décédé en 1305, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Coimbra, Portugal.

de.lifeSummary: Als Martim Vasques da Cunha 1235 in Coimbra, Portugal geboren wurde, war sein Vater Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua 40 und seine Mutter Teresa Pires Portel 25. Er hatte mit Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1305 im Alter von 70 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Coimbra, Portugal begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Martim Vasques da Cunha è nato il nel 1235 nel Coimbra, Portogallo, il suo padre, Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Teresa Pires Portel, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie con Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães. Morì nel 1305, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Coimbra, Portogallo.

ja.Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal: ポルトガルブラガブラガクウンハ
ja.Concelho de Tábua, Distrito de Coimbra,: ポルトガルコインブラ
ja.lifeSummary: Martim Vasques da Cunhaが1235年にポルトガルコインブラで生まれた時，父Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábuaは40歳，母Teresa Pires Portelは25歳であった。彼はJoana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomãesとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1305年に故郷で70歳で死去し，ポルトガルコインブラにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Martim Vasques da Cunha(이)가 1235년 포르투갈 코임브라에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Teresa Pires Portel(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1305년 그의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 포르투갈 코임브라에 묻혔다.

historicEvents.title: Portugal
historicEvents.description: Portugal is one of the oldest nations in Europe having established its continental frontier as it is set today in 1297

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Martim Vasques da Cunha nasceu em 1235, em Coimbra, Portugal, seu pai, Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Teresa Pires Portel, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas com Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães. Ele faleceu em 1305, em sua cidade natal, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Coimbra, Portugal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Martim Vasques da Cunha родился в 1235 г. в местности коимбра, Португалия, его отцу по имени Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Teresa Pires Portel, 25. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери от Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães. Он умер в 1305 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности коимбра, Португалия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Martim Vasques da Cunha nació en 1235, en Coimbra, Portugal, su papá, Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Teresa Pires Portel, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas con Joana Rodrigues Ruiz de Nomães. Murió en 1305, en su pueblo, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Coimbra, Portugal.
